Technology & Equipment

State-of-the-Art Machinery

Our workspace is equipped with advanced machinery and digital tools for precision manufacturing, finishing, and design. Explore the technology that powers our premium furniture production.

Precision cutting and intricate carving for custom furniture and doors.

Fully coordinated workspace for seamless production workflow.

Our Story

Craftsmanship
Built to Last

Interior Duct Ltd is a company that manufactures top-notch modern furniture of all types, size and fashion, to make your life more comfortable. The company was renamed on 10th April, 2024 by a Special Resolution of the Board from its previously incorporated “Interior Components Limited” which was born on 6th February 2012 with the intention to meet the growing demand for bespoke furniture that competes favourably with international standard.

Today, we operate a state-of-the-art manufacturing facility and serve clients nationwide across Nigeria — delivering everything from single bespoke pieces to complete hotel fit-outs.

Since commencement of business on 27th April, 2023, our focus has been on “Functionality, Durability and Aesthetics to bring about customer satisfaction and create value for their investments. At Interior Duct Ltd, our products are all designed and fabricated under qualified supervision of experts with a great variety of finishing. We build our products from a wide range of high quality materials sourced locally and from around the world. This broad variety of materials gives us the added flexibility to produce an extensive array of quality furniture in Nigeria. We design, produce and market an extensive range of quality furniture that serve residential, office, commercial (hotels, restaurants, etc), educational (schools, colleges, universities, etc), institutional, and medical clients (hospitals, labs, etc).

Our Mission

Creating a meaningful living atmosphere in every sphere where our products are applicable.

Our Vision

Building world-class brand and improving local content using environment-friendly practices.

About the Founder

Benedict Omoregbe Onaiwu

Director & Chief Executive Officer

Mr. Benedict Omoregbe Onaiwu, the Director/Chief Executive Officer incorporated the company as a fresh graduate of Petroleum Engineering with the passion to ingeniously create furniture pieces from virtually all materials he could lay his hand upon. Back then, he conceived the idea to start a business that caters for the demand for specialized and foreign furniture products. The need for economic viability and business sustenance led him to embrace the general furniture brands to solve the prevalent problems associated with importation of modern furniture products. A drive that later birthed Interior Components Limited on 7th February, 2012.

Totally dissatisfied with the level of attention to details and waste of natural resources, he began researches and acquired industrial machines/tools for experiment on running a furniture production system that could make use of about 96 percent of wood and other materials sourced into wood workshop. This led to the creation of light products like jewelry boxes, magazine rack and other basic racking systems for personal use.

Fueled by the developing passion for expansion, excellence and minimizing waste, he has pursued a self-training programme to explore the use of the right and versatile tools/machines to achieve the company’s high rate of productive and premium finish.

In addition to the pursuit to inspire the spirit of professionalism and awaken interest in furniture craftsmanship, a special training scheme (Catch Them Young) for teens and the younger generation, has been initiated since 2017 as part of his philanthropic activities to the society through his NG Bomon Development Foundation. In his personal time, he loves to play music and spend time with his lovely wife and children.

The Director’s efforts to expand his philanthropic give-back to the community led to the accreditation of the Company by the National Board for Technical Education (NBTE) as an approved Skill Training Centre (STC), to train young people on Furniture/Upholstery and Welding/Fabrications, most of whom are currently receiving vocational training on full scholarship awarded by the Company. Collaborated with ITF SUPA programme to trained both intending and skilled artisans for self-reliance and decent sources of livelihood as a means of poverty and prevalence crimes reduction in our societies.

The company is inclusive and non-discriminative, beneficiaries of all walks of life, especially indigents has also been sponsored on different levels of academic scholarships both in elementary schools and tertiary institutions within the community the company operates.

Environmental Commitment

Interior Duct Ltd is not only committed to making wonderful furniture, in alignment with the SDGs; precisely the Goals 5, 8, 9, 12 and 13 (Gender Equality, Decent Work & Economic Growth, Industry Innovation & Infrastructure, Responsible Consumption & Production and Climate Action respectively), we are also committed to the sustainability of our planet’s limited resources by striving to reduce, reuse and recycle manufacturing bye-products and other creative solutions to mitigate noxious gases emission that may build up in confined working spaces in all our production processes. This is in a bit to conserve natural resources for a common good.
